summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

actions.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit with before/after file-system snapshots, SHA-256 digest comparison, and PRD readiness integration that blocks on project mutations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates SHA-256 digests of all project files using git ls-files or filesystem walk; diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after states to detect added/modified/deleted files.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation proves file-system mutation detection capability exists.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime captures boundary snapshot before agent execution, runs all evidence tasks, captures after snapshot, computes diff, and includes boundary.changes in result.
evidence.why: Runtime execution flow proves boundary audit is integrated into the dispatch pipeline.

evidence.summary: result.boundary includes project_mutation status, allowed_write_roots, and changes array with file paths and change types.
evidence.why: Dispatch result schema proves boundary audit output structure is defined and returned.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations map to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and are appended to readiness.blockers, forcing prd_ready=false when changes exist.
evidence.why: Integration with readiness proves boundary violations block PRD progress.

evidence.summary: execution_policy declares writes_business_code=false, agent_instruction='boundary_controlled', and boundary with project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots array.
evidence.why: Execution policy schema proves boundary restrictions are declared before agent execution.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt injects boundary rules into agent instructions: 'do not modify target project files', 'allowed artifact/scratch root', 'return JSON to stdout, harness captures and writes artifacts'.
evidence.why: Agent prompt generation proves boundary instructions are communicated to evidence agents.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agentToolProfile to 'boundary', 'research', or 'full'; 'full' requires allowFullAgentTools=true else downgrades to 'boundary'; Claude dispatch clears tools/settings flags to rely on boundary enforcement.
evidence.why: Tool profile normalization proves 'boundary' is default and 'full' requires explicit authorization.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code=false and specific objectives for finding facts, cross-checking, and verifying evidence.
evidence.why: Evidence agent protocol definitions prove all three roles are defined as non-mutating.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks each required evidence role for completed results, calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues/evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues/projectEvidenceBlockingIssues, and adds blockers for missing/invalid results.
evidence.why: Readiness inspection proves evidence agent results are validated before PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates agent writing unexpected-project-write.txt and asserts status='blocked', boundary.project_mutation='violated', and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Test proves boundary audit detects mutations and blocks readiness even with passing agent results.

evidence.summary: Tests 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' and 'gitignored boundary' prove git ls-files integration detects additions/modifications even for gitignored files like .env.
evidence.why: Tests prove boundary audit works with git repositories and detects various mutation types.

evidence.summary: test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' proves filesystem walk fallback detects modifications while excluding writes within runtimeDir (allowed_write_roots).
evidence.why: Test proves boundary audit handles non-git repos and correctly allows artifact writes.

evidence.summary: test 'claude full tool profile requires explicit authorization' proves agent_tool_profile='full' without allowFullAgentTools=true downgrades to 'boundary' and clears tool restriction flags.
evidence.why: Test proves full tool profile requires explicit authorization and boundary enforcement is the default.

evidence.summary: gitFiles uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' for tracked/untracked files; walkFiles fallback walks directory tree excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache.
evidence.why: File enumeration implementation proves boundary audit excludes common build/cache directories.

risks: If boundary snapshot is slow on large repos, evidence dispatch timeout may miss mutations that occur after snapshot.
If allowed_write_roots is misconfigured to include project directories, mutations could be allowed incorrectly.

result.notes: Evidence dispatch implements comprehensive boundary audit with git and filesystem fallbacks, tool profile normalization, and PRD readiness integration that blocks on mutations. Test coverage confirms violations block readiness even when agents report success.

agent_results.claim: Confirmed: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit with before/after file-system snapshots, SHA-256 digest comparison, git and filesystem fallbacks, tool profile normalization, and PRD readiness integration that blocks on project mutations.

evidence.summary: fileDigest creates SHA-256 digest using node:crypto createHash('sha256').update(readFileSync(path)).digest('hex').
evidence.why: Independently confirms SHA-256 digest implementation matches explorer claim.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot uses git ls-files or walkFiles to enumerate project files, then calls fileDigest for each file outside allowedRoots.
evidence.why: Confirms snapshot creation combines file enumeration with SHA-256 digests.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots iterates before/after Map entries to detect deleted, modified, and added files, returning sorted changes array.
evidence.why: Confirms diff algorithm detects all three mutation types.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime captures boundaryBefore before agent loop, boundaryAfter after loop, then computes boundaryChanges via diffBoundarySnapshots.
evidence.why: Confirms before/after snapshot timing and diff integration in runtime execution.

evidence.summary: result.boundary includes project_mutation status, allowed_write_roots array, and changes array with path and change type for each mutation.
evidence.why: Confirms boundary audit result structure provides violation details.

evidence.summary: boundaryBlockers map changes to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers, which are appended to readiness.blockers and force prd_ready=false when violations exist.
evidence.why: Confirms boundary violations directly block PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: gitFiles uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' for tracked/untracked files; walkFiles excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache directories.
evidence.why: Confirms file enumeration uses git when available and excludes common build/cache directories.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt injects boundary rules: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files', allowed artifact root, 'harness captures stdout and writes artifacts', and project root paths.
evidence.why: Confirms agents receive explicit boundary instructions in prompts.

evidence.summary: execution_policy declares writes_business_code=false, agent_instruction='boundary_controlled', and boundary with project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots.
evidence.why: Confirms boundary restrictions are declared in execution policy before agent execution.

evidence.summary: For claude provider, agentToolProfile normalizes to 'research' for research-related inputs, 'full' only when allowFullAgentTools=true, else 'boundary'; clears claude_tools and settings flags.
evidence.why: Confirms tool profile normalization and boundary enforcement as default.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code=false and objectives for finding facts, cross-checking, and verifying.
evidence.why: Confirms all evidence agent roles are defined as non-mutating.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness iterates requiredEvidenceRoles, checks evidenceResultComplete, and calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues, evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues, and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues.
evidence.why: Confirms readiness inspection validates evidence agent results before PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates agent write, asserts status='blocked', boundary.project_mutation='violated', and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Confirms test proves mutations block readiness despite passing agent results.

evidence.summary: Tests 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' and 'gitignored boundary' prove git ls-files integration detects additions/modifications including .env.
evidence.why: Confirms tests prove git-based file enumeration detects various mutation types.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' proves filesystem walk detects modifications while excluding runtimeDir writes.
evidence.why: Confirms test proves non-git fallback and artifact exclusion work correctly.

evidence.summary: Test 'claude full tool profile requires explicit authorization' proves agent_tool_profile='full' without allowFullAgentTools=true downgrades to 'boundary'.
evidence.why: Confirms test proves full tool profile requires explicit authorization.

result.notes: All explorer claims independently verified via direct file reads. Boundary audit implementation is comprehensive with SHA-256 digests, git/filesystem fallbacks, tool profile normalization, agent prompt injection, and PRD readiness integration. Test coverage confirms violations block readiness even when agents report success.

agent_results.claim: Verified: Explorer and cross-checker independently confirmed that YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit with before/after file-system snapshots, SHA-256 digest comparison, git and filesystem fallbacks, tool profile normalization, and PRD readiness integration that blocks on project mutations. All evidence is project-scoped with verifiable paths, no assumptions presented as facts, and test coverage confirms boundary violations block PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both cited buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots as implementing SHA-256 digest comparison with before/after file-system snapshots.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence paths are consistent across independent agents and implementation exists.

evidence.summary: Both agents cited boundaryBlockers mapping changes to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers appended to readiness.blockers.
evidence.why: Verifies PRD readiness integration is independently confirmed.

evidence.summary: Both agents cited test suite proving boundary blocks mutations even when agent results pass, with git and filesystem fallbacks.
evidence.why: Verifies test coverage is independently confirmed and supports acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: Both agents confirmed gitFiles and walkFiles enumeration with build cache exclusions.
evidence.why: Verifies file enumeration implementation is independently verified.

evidence.summary: Both agents cited DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS and inspectDemandPrdReadiness.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence agent protocols and readiness inspection are independently confirmed.

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker achieved evidence agreement with no conflicts. All 15 evidence records are project-scoped with verifiable paths/lines. No assumptions promoted to facts. Test coverage confirms boundary violations block PRD readiness. Acceptance criteria met: explorer and cross-checker cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD blocked on mutations. Ready for PRD.
